details: The agent ran comprehensive kubectl commands against the live cluster and correctly identified all 11 fault namespaces with accurate root-cause categories: (1) fault-configmap: missing ConfigMap 'app-config'; (2) fault-crashloop: database connection refused causing app exit; (3) fault-endpoints: service selector mismatch (app=web-backend-v2 vs actual label app=web-backend); (4) fault-imagepull: invalid image tag nginx:1.99.99-nonexistent; (5) fault-init: init container failing with migration table locked error; (6) fault-multicontainer: log-shipper sidecar cannot resolve DNS for log-collector.logging.svc; (7) fault-oom: OOMKilled with 25Mi memory limit too low; (8) fault-pvc: StorageClass 'fast-ssd' doesn't exist, PVC unbound; (9) fault-readiness: readiness probe targeting port 8080 but nginx listening on 80; (10) fault-rollout: stalled rollout with ProgressDeadlineExceeded due to invalid image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist; (11) fault-unschedulable: nodeSelector disktype=ssd-nvme-gen5 with no matching nodes. The healthy namespace was correctly not flagged. All diagnoses are supported by actual command output evidence.
